What’s on This Week

The weekly “What’s On” list features upcoming live music events in Vancouver, Burnaby, North Vancouver, West Vancouver, and Port Moody. We also maintain a current list of weekly jams and open mics on this page.

While most of the live music we list is free or has a minimal cover charge, our “Shows of Note” section will highlight ticketed live concerts at larger venues (usually under 500 capacity) with full production, within the genres ShowHub features. We spotlight these shows because they are likely to sell out, giving audiences a chance to discover and plan for premium live music experiences in advance. If there are no relevant shows upcoming, the section is hidden until more are listed.

Upcoming Live Music

    Friday, Mar 13 at 7:00 PM

Weekly Jams

Please check the links or contact the venue to make sure these are happening before heading out. We review them periodically but sometimes things change without us catching it. Also, many do not happen on holidays / stats.

  • ANAF 100 Jam (East Van)
    Mondays 7 – 11 PM
    Except holidays
  • Begbies Open Mic Jam (New West)
    Mondays 7 – 11 PM
  • Pacific Bluegrass Jam at the ANZA (Fairview)
    Mondays 7 PM – Midnight
  • Romer’s (River District, Vancouver)
    Open Mic Every Monday
    With in-house backline
  • Great Bear Pub Open Jam (Burnaby)
    Mondays 9 PM – Midnight
  • Open Mic at Rocky Point Spirits (Port Moody)
    Tuesdays 6 – 9 PM
  • Georgies Jam (New West)
    1st and last Tuesdays of the month 7 PM
    Rotating Hosts – Dates may vary. Check link for details
  • End of the Line Jam at the Princeton (East Van)
    Tuesdays 8:30 PM – 12:30 AM
    Hosted by Sandy Bone and Double D
  • The Village Pub (South Van – Champlain Heights)
    Wednesdays 6:30 – 10:30 PM
  • Pioneers Pub (Richmond)
    Wednesdays 7 – 10 PM
  • Blues Jam at the ANZA club (Fairview)
    Every 1st & 3rd Wednesday 7 – 11 PM
  • The Gander Jam at Lana Lou’s (DTES)
    Every 1st & 3rd Wednesday 8:30 PM – Midnight
  • Shady Island Bar & Grill Jam (Richmond)
    Thursdays 6 – 9 PM
    Hosted by the Aristo-Cats
  • Moose’s Down Under – Jazz Jam (Downtown)
    Thursdays 7 – 10 PM
    Hosted by the Cameron Hood Trio with rotating guests
    $10 cover for non-performers
  • Trees Open Mic (Granville – downtown)
    Thursdays 7 – 10 PM
    Hosted by Marq DeSouza
  • Commercial Drive Legion Jam (East Van)
    Thursdays 7 – 11 PM
  • The Painted Ship Open Mic (Kits)
    Thursdays 7:30 – 11:30 PM
  • Eagles Club Open Mic (North Van)
    Thursdays 8 – 11 PM
    Rotating Hosts
  • Mike Van Eyes at Lana Lou’s (DTES)
    Saturdays 4 – 7 PM
    Special guest pro set followed by jam.
    Cover charge applies to all.
  • Fraser Valley Blues Society Jam at the Eagles Hall (Abbotsford)
    Second Friday of each month: 7 – 11 PM
    Cover $10 for those not performing
    (double check for holiday weekends)
  • Admiral Jazz Jam (Burnaby)
    Sundays 1 – 4 PM
  • Lynn Valley Legion Jam (North Van)
    Last Sunday of the month 3 – 6 PM
    House band with rotating guests
    Sunday roast available starting at 4pm
  • Ivanhoe Jam (DTES)
    Sundays 4 – 8 PM
  • Donegals (Surrey)
    Sundays 5 – 9 PM
  • West Van Legion Jam (West Van)
    Most Sundays 6:30 PM
    Check calendar at the link for details
  • Georgie’s Open Mic (New West)
    Sundays 6 PM
    Hosted by Lana Crockett

ShowHub listings are free for small-to-mid-size venues, local artists, and community events. This ad-free platform is developed, curated, and run by two locals who care about our music scene. If you find ShowHub useful and would like to help us support local artists and venues, we invite you to explore ways to get involved or make a contribution.

ShowHub is made possible by:

Supportive Partner:

Community Partners:

ShowHub is supported by Creative BC and the Province of British Columbia

ShowHub respectfully acknowledges that both the ShowHub founder residences and the shows we are listing are located, or will take place on, the traditional, unceded, and ancestral territories of the Coast Salish First Nations peoples, including the səl̓ilwətaɁɬ təməxʷ, Skwxwú7mesh-ulh Temíx̱w, and xʷməθkʷəy̓əm.
